description: Helper-voice welcome-back screen for returning users + task lifecycle (auto-snooze, bring-back, completion inference). Leads with what the app did for the user, surfaces at most one ask, suppresses entirely during live meetings. Built around the principle: a returning user should feel helped, not nagged.

userStories.title: Persist lastOpenedAt + welcomeBackLastSeenAt timestamps in config
userStories.description: As a developer, I need two timestamps tracked in config so welcome-back logic has reliable gap-since-last-open and last-dismissed signals.
acceptanceCriteria: Extend Config in src/main/config.ts with lastOpenedAt: string | null and welcomeBackLastSeenAt: string | null; defaults null
Write lastOpenedAt = new Date().toISOString() on BOTH mainWindow 'ready-to-show' and mainWindow 'show' events (covers first open AND tray → show)
Export helper getDaysSinceLastOpen(): number | null — returns null when lastOpenedAt is null (first-ever launch)
Export helper markWelcomeBackSeen(): void that writes welcomeBackLastSeenAt = new Date().toISOString()
On fresh install with no prior lastOpenedAt, downstream code treats as first-ever open (no welcome-back)
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Task snooze schema + helpers (snoozedAt, snoozedReason, lastMentionedAt)
userStories.description: As a developer, I need task records to support a soft snoozed state with full reversibility so the staleness sweep and filter UI have a stable substrate.
acceptanceCriteria: Extend task records in src/main/database.ts with three new fields: snoozedAt: string | null, snoozedReason: string | null, lastMentionedAt: string | null
Add helper snoozeTask(taskId: string, reason: string): Promise<void> — sets snoozedAt=now, snoozedReason=reason, bumps updatedAt
Add helper bringBackTask(taskId: string): Promise<void> — clears snoozedAt and snoozedReason, bumps updatedAt
Add helper getSnoozedTasks(): Promise<Task[]>
Add helper touchLastMentioned(taskId: string, when: string): Promise<void>
Add utility isSnoozed(t: Task): boolean (snoozedAt != null)
Modify existing getTasks() to filter out snoozed tasks by default
Add getTasks({ includeSnoozed: true }) overload that returns all (snoozed + active)
Typecheck passes
Tests pass: unit tests for snooze → default getTasks excludes it; bringBack → reappears; touchLastMentioned persists; getSnoozedTasks returns only snoozed

userStories.title: Staleness sweep service — auto-snooze low-priority inactive tasks
userStories.description: As a user, when I come back after a long gap, I want the app to have quietly cleared tasks that clearly aren't relevant anymore so my active list isn't cluttered with ghosts.
acceptanceCriteria: New module src/main/staleness-sweep.ts exporting sweepStaleTasks(): Promise<{ snoozed: Task[] }>
Eligibility criteria (ALL must be true): status === 'active', !isSnoozed(t), updatedAt < now - 30 days, (!lastMentionedAt || lastMentionedAt < now - 14 days), priority !== 'high'
For each eligible task, call snoozeTask(t.id, 'stale-30d')
Return { snoozed: [...just-snoozed tasks...] } for downstream consumption
Call from app.whenReady() AFTER calendar sync finishes (non-blocking — not awaited inside whenReady)
Stash result on module-local lastSweepResult with timestamp so US-004 can read without re-running
Log line: 'staleness-sweep | snoozed=N eligible=E of total active tasks=T'
Typecheck passes
Tests pass: one unit test per eligibility gate (age, mention, priority, already-snoozed) — each returns empty when gate fails

userStories.title: Welcome-back compute IPC — returns ranked buckets for the UI
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a single IPC that returns the entire ranked bucket list so the welcome-back UI component is presentation-only and testable against fixtures.
acceptanceCriteria: Add IPC handler welcomeBack:compute in src/main/main.ts returning either null (don't show) or an object with shape: { gapDays, wins: { cleared?, jiraProgress?, meetingsMatched?, calendarHealthy? }, ask?: { kind: 'contradiction' | 'overdueWithSignal' | 'launchAtStartupOffer', payload } }
Return null when getDaysSinceLastOpen() < 2 OR (welcomeBackLastSeenAt !== null && welcomeBackLastSeenAt >= lastOpenedAt)
wins.cleared pulls from staleness-sweep's lastSweepResult: { count, sampleTitles (up to 3) }
wins.jiraProgress counts Jira stories whose status moved forward (to Done or later-stage) in the gap window: { count, doneCount }
wins.meetingsMatched: number of meetings automatically linked to Jira issues in the gap (from task records or pipeline:jira-auto-push log correlation)
wins.calendarHealthy: upcomingCount for the next 7 days from calendarWatcher.getUpcomingEvents()

Ask-selection priority (pick first that qualifies): (1) kind='contradiction' if detectContradictions has any with createdAt > lastOpenedAt; (2) kind='overdueWithSignal' if an overdue task has lastMentionedAt within 14 days; (3) kind='launchAtStartupOffer' if app.getLoginItemSettings().openAtLogin is false AND gap >= 3 days AND missedMeetingsCount >= 3
If no ask qualifies, ask is omitted
Add IPC welcomeBack:dismiss that calls markWelcomeBackSeen()
Typecheck passes
Tests pass: unit tests for each ask-selection branch + for each null-return condition

userStories.title: Welcome-back UI component — helper-voice rendering, one ask max
userStories.description: As a returning user, I want one scannable screen that shows what the app did for me, with at most one thing I need to act on, phrased as help rather than homework.
acceptanceCriteria: New component src/renderer/WelcomeBack.tsx rendered conditionally from App.tsx on app start
On mount: call inwiseAPI.welcomeBackCompute(). If null, render nothing. If non-null, render the screen

Wins copy (helper voice, count-not-list): cleared → 'Cleared N tasks you hadn\'t touched recently (bring any back anytime)'; jiraProgress → 'N Jira stories moved forward while you were out — M are now Done'; meetingsMatched → 'Matched N new meetings to Jira issues automatically'; calendarHealthy → 'Calendar in sync; N upcoming this week'
ask.contradiction renders: 'One thing worth a look. [summary]. [Review] [Dismiss]'
ask.overdueWithSignal renders: 'One task is past due and you\'ve mentioned it recently: {title}. [Snooze to next week] [Mark done] [Keep as-is]'
ask.launchAtStartupOffer renders: 'Want Inwise to start automatically when you log in? You missed a few meetings while it was closed. [Turn on] [Not now]' (action logic wired in US-009)
When ask is absent, render single line: 'Nothing urgent while you were out — everything\'s where you left it.'
Chip row at bottom: Tasks, Meetings, Jira, Calendar — each navigates to the respective view
[Done] button top-right calls inwiseAPI.welcomeBackDismiss() and unmounts the component
NEVER render more than one ask; NEVER render a list of task/meeting titles (count is the message, detail on-demand via chips)
Typecheck passes

Verify in browser using dev-browser skill: launch app with synthetic welcomeBack:compute payloads covering (a) wins-only, (b) ask.contradiction, (c) empty-state (no wins, no ask); confirm copy matches; confirm dismiss persists welcomeBackLastSeenAt

userStories.title: Snoozed tasks filter + [Bring back] UI in the Tasks view
userStories.description: As a user, when I want to review what the app auto-snoozed, I can see those tasks under a filter and bring any back with one click.
acceptanceCriteria: In src/renderer/MyTasks.tsx add a filter pill/tab labeled 'Snoozed' alongside existing filters
Selecting Snoozed calls inwiseAPI.getSnoozedTasks() (new IPC wrapper) and renders the returned tasks
Each snoozed row displays: title, original due date (if any), snoozedAt (formatted relative, e.g. '3 days ago'), snoozedReason in human form (e.g., 'auto-snoozed — no activity for 30+ days')
[Bring back] button per row calls inwiseAPI.bringBackTask(id); row disappears from the Snoozed list and reappears in Active
[Bring back all] button at the top of the Snoozed view; confirms with a quick inline summary ('Brought back 5 tasks')
Inline confirmation toast/flash for 3 seconds after each bring-back: 'Brought back — back in your active list.'
Badge on the Snoozed filter pill shows the count when > 0; hidden when 0
Typecheck passes

Verify in browser using dev-browser skill: seed 3 snoozed tasks, navigate to Snoozed filter, confirm all 3 render with reasons; bring one back, confirm it disappears from Snoozed and appears in Active; bring back all, confirm empty state

userStories.title: Task completion inference from transcripts (flag likely-done, never auto-complete)
userStories.description: As a user, after a meeting is transcribed, I want the app to flag tasks it thinks I completed based on what I said, so I can confirm with one click — but it never auto-closes a task.
acceptanceCriteria: In runRecordingPipeline in src/main/main.ts, after saveInsights and before pipeline:done, add step inferCompletedTasks(transcript, openTasks)
The step takes the meeting transcript and the current user's active, non-snoozed tasks, makes ONE LLM call via the configured apiProvider (existing extractor pattern)
LLM prompt must require high confidence — prefer false negatives over false positives; return only task IDs whose completion the transcript strongly implies
For each returned task ID, set likelyDone: true on the task (extend schema with new boolean field, default false)
DO NOT set status: 'done' automatically
Log line: pipeline:likely-done | flagged=N tasks (list task IDs at debug level)
In the Tasks view (MyTasks.tsx), tasks with likelyDone === true render a 'Done?' pill beside the title with [Yes] [No] inline actions
[Yes] sets status='done', clears likelyDone, bumps updatedAt
[No] clears likelyDone only, bumps updatedAt (signals user-rejection)
Only evaluate transcripts within 24h of their creation; skip for older ones
Typecheck passes

Tests pass: unit tests for the inference call with mocked LLM responses covering (a) empty list, (b) single match, (c) multi-match; UI test that the Done? pill renders when likelyDone === true and clears on [Yes]/[No]

userStories.title: Live-meeting suppression: banner instead of welcome-back when app opens mid-meeting
userStories.description: As a user, if I open the app while I'm currently in a scheduled meeting, I want the app to offer to start recording instead of showing a retrospective welcome screen.
acceptanceCriteria: On app show, BEFORE rendering WelcomeBack, check calendarWatcher.getUpcomingEvents() for an event where startTime <= now <= (endTime || startTime + 90min) AND !isRecordingActive AND overlayWindow is null/destroyed
If such an event exists, suppress WelcomeBack for this session

Render a compact banner at the top of Home (new src/renderer/LiveMeetingBanner.tsx or inline in App.tsx): 'This looks like your meeting with {attendee or event title} — want me to start recording? [Start recording] [Not now]'
[Start recording] triggers existing recording:start IPC with the event's calendarEventId and title
[Not now] dismisses the banner for this specific event id (store in a session-local Set of dismissed event ids); reopening the app later in the same event window does not re-prompt
WelcomeBack becomes eligible again on the NEXT app open after the event ends, not this one
Typecheck passes

Verify in browser using dev-browser skill: inject a synthetic in-progress calendar event via test IPC, confirm banner appears and WelcomeBack is suppressed; click Start, confirm overlay opens; dismiss, confirm banner disappears and does not re-appear for that event id

userStories.title: Launch-at-startup offer (wires ask.launchAtStartupOffer into a one-tap action)
userStories.description: As a user, if I've missed meetings because the app wasn't running, I want a one-tap way to have it launch at login so I don't miss them next time.
acceptanceCriteria: In src/main/main.ts add IPC app:setLoginItemOpenAtLogin(enabled: boolean) that calls app.setLoginItemSettings({ openAtLogin: enabled }) and returns { ok: true } on success
In WelcomeBack.tsx (US-005), when ask.kind === 'launchAtStartupOffer', render the offer card described in US-005
[Turn on] calls inwiseAPI.setLoginItemOpenAtLogin(true); on success, card morphs into a confirmation line: 'Done. Inwise will start automatically next time you log in.'
[Not now] dismisses the card only (sets a component-local flag); it doesn't dismiss the entire welcome-back screen

Missed-meetings detection for US-004's compute: calendar events with startTime > lastOpenedAt AND startTime < now that have NO corresponding row in meetings.db (or only a row with status === 'calendar_sync' — meaning nothing was recorded)
Guard in US-004: if app.getLoginItemSettings().openAtLogin is already true, never return ask.launchAtStartupOffer regardless of missed-meeting count
Typecheck passes

Verify in browser using dev-browser skill: with openAtLogin=false and 3+ simulated missed events, confirm offer renders; click Turn on, confirm success state; re-open app, confirm openAtLogin is now true and offer no longer shows
